task_text: Prepare a comprehensive project health briefing for NovaBridge's leadership team. The briefing should cover: current progress, key risks, upcoming Q3 commitments, and any issues that need leadership attention. Save it as project_health_briefing.txt.

Project Helios — Weekly Status Update
Week of 2026-07-06

Overall: ON TRACK (minor risks)

Milestone Progress:
- Data ingestion module: 85% complete
- API gateway: 100% complete (delivered)
- Client dashboard: 60% complete
- Integration testing: 40% complete

Sprint 14 Goals:
- Complete dashboard module v1
- Resolve data format inconsistencies (Blocker #1)
- Begin UAT preparation

Open Blockers:
1. Meridian source data contains inconsistent date formats (ISO 8601 vs. MM/DD/YYYY).
   Dev team awaiting clarification from Meridian IT. Estimated delay: 2-3 days.
2. Third-party mapping library (GeoSync v2.1) license renewal pending. Legal reviewing.
   Target resolution: 2026-07-14.

Next milestone: Integration Test Sign-off — target 2026-07-25

Project Helios — Q2 Deliverables Checklist

[DONE]      Requirements documentation v1.0
[DONE]      System architecture design
[DONE]      API gateway implementation
[DONE]      Development environment setup
[DONE]      Data schema finalization
[IN-PROGRESS] Data ingestion module (target: 2026-07-18)
[IN-PROGRESS] Client dashboard v1 (target: 2026-07-22)
[OVERDUE]   Integration test suite setup (was due 2026-06-30; rescheduled to 2026-07-20)
[OVERDUE]   UAT environment provisioning (was due 2026-07-01; blocked by Meridian IT access)
[OVERDUE]   Performance benchmarking report (was due 2026-07-05; owner: Derek Okafor)

Project Helios — Q3 Roadmap
INTERNAL DRAFT — Not for client distribution
Last updated: 2026-07-06

Q3 2026 Priorities:

1. COMPLETE SYSTEM INTEGRATION (July)
   - Finalize data ingestion module (target: 2026-07-18)
   - Complete client dashboard v1 (target: 2026-07-22)
   - Integration test sign-off (target: 2026-07-25)

2. PRODUCT DEMO FOR MERIDIAN (July 25)
   - Live data ingestion demonstration using Meridian sample data
   - End-to-end pipeline walkthrough
   - Dashboard visualization with real data (per contract Section 9.3)

3. USER ACCEPTANCE TESTING — MERIDIAN GROUP (August 17-28)
   - Deploy to UAT environment
   - Support Meridian user testing sessions
   - Address UAT feedback (budget: 2 sprints)

4. PERFORMANCE HARDENING (August-September)
   - Dashboard load time target: <2 seconds for 100k records
   - Ingestion throughput benchmarking (target: 500k records/min sustained)
   - External security penetration test (vendor TBD)

5. LAUNCH PREPARATION (September)
   - Production environment setup and runbook documentation
   - Hypercare plan finalization (30-day post-launch model)
   - Final client training sessions

Target launch date: 2026-10-01
Key risk: UAT slippage of >2 weeks pushes launch into Q4, triggering Section 7.1 penalty review.

Proposed Q3 feature additions (subject to change control approval):
- Scheduled ingestion jobs (cron-based triggers)
- Email alerting for pipeline failures
- Self-service mapping rule editor (deferred from Q2)

Project Helios — Risk Register
Last reviewed: 2026-07-07 | Owner: Sarah Chen (PM)

ID    Risk Description                             Likelihood  Impact  Score  Mitigation Owner    Status
----  -------------------------------------------  ----------  ------  -----  ------------------  --------
R-01  Meridian source data quality issues           High        High    9      Amir Hassan         Active — date format blocker confirmed in sprint 14
R-02  GeoSync license not renewed before demo       Medium      High    6      Raj Patel           Active — legal review in progress, due 2026-07-14
R-03  Dashboard performance on large datasets       Medium      High    6      Derek Okafor        Active — benchmark task HEL-247 not yet started
R-04  UAT timeline slippage                        High        Medium  6      Sarah Chen          Active — 1-week extension already consumed
R-05  Key person dependency: Derek Okafor           Low         High    3      Sarah Chen          Monitored — no backup tech lead identified
R-06  Meridian IT environment access delays        Medium      Medium  4      Nadia Petrov        Active — awaiting IT infrastructure diagram
R-07  Scope creep from Meridian feature requests   Low         Medium  2      Raj Patel           Monitored — 3 informal requests logged, not yet escalated
R-08  Cloud cost overrun                           Low         Low     1      Nadia Petrov        Monitored
R-09  Third-party API deprecation (non-GeoSync)    Very Low    Medium  1      Olivia Brooks       Monitored

Meeting Notes: Project Helios / Meridian Group Sync
Date: 2026-07-08
Attendees: Sarah Chen (NovaBridge PM), Derek Okafor (NovaBridge Tech Lead),
           Priya Kapoor (Meridian IT Lead), James Whitfield (Meridian Project Sponsor)

Decisions:
1. Demo date confirmed: 2026-07-25 (product demo for Meridian stakeholders)
2. Meridian will provide clean sample dataset by 2026-07-15 to unblock date format issue
3. UAT period extended by 1 week (now Aug 17-28)

Action Items:
- NovaBridge: Resolve GeoSync license before demo (owner: Derek Okafor, due 2026-07-14)
- NovaBridge: Send draft demo agenda to Meridian by 2026-07-18 (owner: Sarah Chen)
- Meridian: Share IT infrastructure diagram for integration environment setup
  (owner: Priya Kapoor, due 2026-07-12)

Notes:
- James Whitfield emphasized that the demo must include live data ingestion, not mock data.
- Concern raised about dashboard load times on large datasets. Derek to benchmark before demo.
- Priya Kapoor confirmed Meridian IT will provision UAT environment access by 2026-07-19.

input.content:
Project Health Briefing — Project Helios / Meridian Group
Prepared for: NovaBridge Leadership Team
Date: 2026-07-11

Executive Summary
Project Helios is currently ON TRACK with minor but material risks. Core foundation work is complete, including requirements, architecture, API gateway, development environment, and data schema finalization. However, several items that were expected by the end of Q2 have slipped into July, and the project is now carrying schedule pressure ahead of the July 25 Meridian product demo and August UAT window.

The most important leadership concern is that multiple dependencies converge before the July 25 demo: clean Meridian sample data, GeoSync license renewal, dashboard performance benchmarking, and integration test readiness. If these are not resolved on time, the demo quality and downstream UAT schedule may be affected. UAT slippage of more than two weeks would push launch into Q4 and could trigger a contract penalty review.

Current Progress
Overall status: ON TRACK, with minor risks.

Completed / Delivered
- Requirements documentation v1.0: complete.
- System architecture design: complete.
- API gateway implementation: complete and delivered.
- Development environment setup: complete.
- Data schema finalization: complete.

In Progress
- Data ingestion module: 85% complete; target completion 2026-07-18.
- Client dashboard v1: 60% complete; target completion 2026-07-22.
- Integration testing: 40% complete; integration test sign-off target 2026-07-25.
- UAT preparation has begun, but environment provisioning remains dependent on Meridian IT access.

Delayed / Overdue Items
- Integration test suite setup: overdue from 2026-06-30; rescheduled to 2026-07-20.
- UAT environment provisioning: overdue from 2026-07-01; blocked by Meridian IT access, with Meridian now targeting access by 2026-07-19.
- Performance benchmarking report: overdue from 2026-07-05; owner Derek Okafor. This is especially important given concerns about dashboard load times on large datasets.

Key Risks
1. Meridian source data quality issues
   - Likelihood: High; Impact: High.
   - Current issue: Meridian source data contains inconsistent date formats, including ISO 8601 and MM/DD/YYYY.
   - Current mitigation: Meridian has committed to providing a clean sample dataset by 2026-07-15.
   - Leadership relevance: This is the top active blocker and may affect live ingestion readiness for the July 25 demo.

2. GeoSync license renewal not completed before demo
   - Likelihood: Medium; Impact: High.
   - Current issue: Third-party mapping library GeoSync v2.1 license renewal is pending legal review.
   - Target resolution: 2026-07-14.
   - Leadership relevance: Delay may compromise demo scope or require a fallback plan.

3. Dashboard performance on large datasets
   - Likelihood: Medium; Impact: High.
   - Current issue: Benchmarking has not yet started, and Meridian has raised concerns about dashboard load times.
   - Q3 target: Dashboard load time under 2 seconds for 100k records.
   - Leadership relevance: Performance gaps could affect client confidence during the demo and UAT.

4. UAT timeline slippage
   - Likelihood: High; Impact: Medium.
   - Current issue: A one-week UAT extension has already been consumed; UAT is now scheduled for 2026-08-17 through 2026-08-28.
   - Leadership relevance: More than two weeks of UAT slippage could push launch into Q4 and trigger Section 7.1 penalty review.

5. Meridian IT environment access delays
   - Likelihood: Medium; Impact: Medium.
   - Current issue: NovaBridge is awaiting Meridian’s IT infrastructure diagram and UAT environment access.
   - Current commitments: Infrastructure diagram due 2026-07-12; UAT environment access expected by 2026-07-19.
   - Leadership relevance: Continued delay may compress integration, testing, and UAT preparation timelines.

6. Key person dependency
   - Likelihood: Low; Impact: High.
   - Current issue: No backup technical lead has been identified for Derek Okafor.
   - Leadership relevance: Derek owns or is directly implicated in several critical-path activities, including performance benchmarking and GeoSync resolution.

Upcoming Q3 Commitments
July 2026
- 2026-07-12: Meridian to provide IT infrastructure diagram for integration environment setup.
- 2026-07-14: Resolve GeoSync license renewal before demo.
- 2026-07-15: Meridian to provide clean sample dataset to unblock date format issue.
- 2026-07-18: Finalize data ingestion module.
- 2026-07-18: Send draft demo agenda to Meridian.
- 2026-07-19: Meridian IT to provision UAT environment access.
- 2026-07-20: Complete rescheduled integration test suite setup.
- 2026-07-22: Complete client dashboard v1.
- 2026-07-25: Product demo for Meridian stakeholders, including live data ingestion using Meridian sample data, end-to-end pipeline walkthrough, and dashboard visualization with real data.
- 2026-07-25: Integration test sign-off target.

August 2026
- 2026-08-17 to 2026-08-28: Meridian user acceptance testing.
- Support Meridian user testing sessions.
- Address UAT feedback within the planned two-sprint budget.
- Begin / continue performance hardening.

August–September 2026
- Performance hardening:
  - Dashboard load time target: under 2 seconds for 100k records.
  - Ingestion throughput target: 500k records per minute sustained.
  - External security penetration test, vendor still to be determined.

September 2026
- Production environment setup.
- Runbook documentation.
- Hypercare plan finalization using a 30-day post-launch model.
- Final client training sessions.

Target Launch
- Planned launch date: 2026-10-01.

Issues Requiring Leadership Attention
1. Ensure critical pre-demo blockers are cleared by mid-July
   - Confirm ownership and urgency for GeoSync license renewal by 2026-07-14.
   - Track Meridian clean sample dataset delivery by 2026-07-15.
   - Confirm demo readiness includes live data ingestion, as explicitly requested by Meridian’s sponsor.

2. Escalate performance benchmarking
   - The performance benchmarking report is overdue and dashboard performance is a client-visible concern.
   - Leadership may need to ensure Derek Okafor has sufficient support or reprioritize work to complete benchmarking before the July 25 demo.

3. Reduce key person dependency risk
   - Derek Okafor is central to technical delivery, performance benchmarking, and GeoSync resolution, with no identified backup technical lead.
   - Leadership should consider assigning a deputy technical lead or backup owner immediately.

4. Monitor Meridian IT dependencies
   - Meridian dependencies remain on the critical path: infrastructure diagram, clean sample data, and UAT access.
   - If Meridian misses the July 12, July 15, or July 19 commitments, NovaBridge may need executive-to-executive escalation to protect the demo and UAT schedule.

5. Maintain scope discipline
   - Proposed Q3 additions, including scheduled ingestion jobs, email alerting, and self-service mapping rule editor, should remain subject to formal change control.
   - Informal Meridian feature requests are currently monitored and should not be allowed to affect demo, UAT, or launch commitments without approved tradeoffs.

Recommended Leadership Actions
- Request a daily critical-path check-in through the July 25 demo covering data readiness, GeoSync licensing, integration testing, dashboard performance, and UAT access.
- Ask Legal / contract stakeholders to confirm the status and expected closure of the GeoSync license renewal by 2026-07-14.
- Direct the delivery team to complete dashboard performance benchmarking before the demo and identify any remediation needed.
- Assign a backup technical lead or named deputy for Derek Okafor before the UAT window.
- Prepare escalation messaging for Meridian if their July dependencies slip.

Overall Health Assessment
Project Helios remains recoverable and broadly on track, but the margin for delay is narrowing. The July 25 demo is the next major confidence point. Successful delivery depends on resolving the data format blocker, completing the GeoSync license renewal, validating performance, and securing Meridian IT access on the currently committed dates.
